get.description:
Returns a string of unaligned nucleotide sequences. Only sequences matching the 
    specified sequence filters are considered.

parameters.name: sequenceFilters
parameters.description: Valid filters for sequence data. This may be empty. Only provide the fields that should be filtered by.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

parameters.name: dataFormat
parameters.description:
The format of the sequences in the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

parameters.name: fastaHeaderTemplate
parameters.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.segment}" to include the segment name in the header.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description:
A UUID that uniquely identifies the request for tracing purposes.
If none is provided in the request, LAPIS will generate one.

parameters.name: downloadAsFile
parameters.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

schema.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

parameters.name: downloadFileBasename
parameters.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

schema.description:

parameters.description:

schema.description:

get.description:
Returns the number of sequences matching the specified sequence filters, grouped by nucleotide mutations.
    Additionally, the relative frequency of each mutation is returned. It is relative to the total number of sequences 
    matching the specified sequence filters with non-ambiguous reads at that position.

parameters.name: sequenceFilters
parameters.description: Valid filters for sequence data. This may be empty. Only provide the fields that should be filtered by.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
The fields of the response to order by.
You can also use \"random\" or \"random(SEED)\" where SEED is an integer.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
The fields that the response items should contain.
If empty, all fields are returned.
If requesting CSV or TSV data, the columns are ordered in the same order as the fields are specified here.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description:
A UUID that uniquely identifies the request for tracing purposes.
If none is provided in the request, LAPIS will generate one.

parameters.name: dataFormat
parameters.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

get.description:
Returns the most recent common ancestor of sequences matching the filter.
    If sequences included in the filter do not exist in the phylogenetic tree they are ignored and the count 
    is added to the field missingNodeCount. To include these sequences in the response set the `printNodesNotInTree`
     parameter to true.

parameters.name: sequenceFilters
parameters.description: Valid filters for sequence data. This may be empty. Only provide the fields that should be filtered by.

parameters.name: phyloTreeField
parameters.description: The field that contains the node labels in the phylogenetic tree.

parameters.name: printNodesNotInTree
parameters.description:
If true, the response will include a comma separated list of nodes that fit the filter criteria 
    but are not present in the phylogenetic tree. The field \"missingNodeCount\" will contain the number of 
    sequences that were not included in the phylogenetic tree.

parameters.name: dataFormat
parameters.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

get.description: Returns the number of sequences matching the specified sequence filters.

parameters.name: sequenceFilters
parameters.description: Valid filters for sequence data. This may be empty. Only provide the fields that should be filtered by.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
The fields to stratify by.
If empty, only the overall count is returned.
If requesting CSV or TSV data, the columns are ordered in the same order as the fields are specified here.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
The fields of the response to order by. 
    Fields specified here must either be \"count\" or also be present in \"fields\".
    You can also use \"random\" or \"random(SEED)\" where SEED is an integer.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

parameters.name: dataFormat
parameters.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

post.description:
Parses a list of advanced query strings into SILO filter expressions.
Returns partial results: successfully parsed queries will have a "filter" field,
while failed queries will have an "error" field with the error message.
The endpoint returns HTTP 200 OK even when some queries fail to parse.
Only malformed request structure (e.g., invalid JSON) will result in HTTP 400 Bad Request.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description:

If none is provided in the request, LAPIS will generate one.

200.description: OK

post.description:
Returns the number of sequences matching the specified queries within the requested date ranges, along 
with the corresponding coverage in a tabular format. The order of the queries and date ranges is preserved.
    
This endpoint is a generalization of the "mutations over time" endpoints. It allows for more complex queries,
such as combinations of mutations ("mutation 1 or mutation 2") and other filters. Since it's not obvious what 
"coverage" means for arbitrary queries, the user always has to specify a coverage query separately.

    with the corresponding coverage in a tabular format. The order of the mutations and date ranges is preserved.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

fields.description:
The fields to stratify by.
If empty, only the overall count is returned.
If requesting CSV or TSV data, the columns are ordered in the same order as the fields are specified here.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

fields.description:
The fields that the response items should contain.
If empty, all fields are returned.
If requesting CSV or TSV data, the columns are ordered in the same order as the fields are specified here.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description: The format of the phylogenetic tree in the response.

phyloTreeField.description: The field that contains the node labels in the phylogenetic tree.

printNodesNotInTree.description:
If true, the response will include a comma separated list of nodes that fit the filter criteria 
    but are not present in the phylogenetic tree. The field \"missingNodeCount\" will contain the number of 
    sequences that were not included in the phylogenetic tree.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.
The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

field.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The format of the sequences in the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

fastaHeaderTemplate.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.gene}" to include the gene name in the header.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.
The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

field.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The format of the sequences in the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

genes.description: List of genes to retrieve sequences for. If not provided, all genes will be returned.

fastaHeaderTemplate.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.gene}" to include the gene name in the header.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

anyOf.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.
The fields by which the result is ordered with ascending or descending order.

field.description: The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.

oneOf.description: Random ordering. Use {random: true} for random order, or {random: SEED} for deterministic random order with a specific seed (where SEED is an integer).
Set to true for random order
Integer seed for deterministic random order

limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

dataFormat.description:
The format of the sequences in the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

fastaHeaderTemplate.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.segment}" to include the segment name in the header.

MutationsOverTimeRequest.description: Request schema for fetching mutations over time.

nucleotideMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

aminoAcidMutations.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of mutations.

items.description:
A amino acid mutation in the format "\<gene\>:\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position". 

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

nucleotideInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description:
A nucleotide insertion in the format "ins_(\<sequenceName\>:)?\<position\>:\<insertion\>".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name.

aminoAcidInsertions.description: Logical "and" concatenation of a list of insertions.

items.description: An amino acid insertion in the format "ins_\<gene\>:\<position\>:\<insertion\>".

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

downloadFileBasename.description:
Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

items.description: Mutation to include

dateRanges.description: List of date ranges for aggregation.

items.description: Date range in which to aggregate mutation data.

dateField.description: Metadata field name containing the date

QueriesOverTimeRequest.description: Request schema for fetching queries over time.

items.description:
A nucleotide mutation in the format "\<sequenceName\>?:\<fromSymbol\>?\<position\>\<toSymbol\>?".  
If the sequenceName is not provided, LAPIS will use the default sequence name. 
The fromSymbol is optional. 
If the toSymbol is not provided, the statement means "has any mutation at the given position".

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

A mutation can be wrapped in a maybe expression "MAYBE(\<mutation\>)"
to include sequences with ambiguous symbols at the given position.

downloadAsFile.description:
Set to true to make your browser trigger a download instead of showing the response content by setting the
'Content-Disposition' header to 'attachment'.

Specify the download file basename, for example, specifying 'myFile' will result in a file named 'myFile.json' 
(when no compression is selected). 
This parameter only takes effect when 'downloadAsFile' is set to true.

compression.description:
Optionally set this to return the response compressed in the specified format.
Alternatively, you can set the 'Accept-Encoding' header to the respective value.

mutation.description: If the genome only contains one segment then this is: (mutationFrom)(position)(mutationTo).If it has more than one segment (e.g., influenza), then the sequence is contained here: (sequenceName):(mutationFrom)(position)(mutationTo)

proportion.description: Number of sequences with this mutation divided by the total number sequences matching the given filter criteria with non-ambiguous reads at that position (i.e. count/coverage).

coverage.description: Total number of sequences with non-ambiguous reads matching the given sequence filter criteria

sequenceName.description: The name of the segment in which the mutation occurs. Null if the genome is single-segmented.

mutationFrom.description: The nucleotide symbol in the reference genome at the position of the mutation

mutationTo.description: The nucleotide symbol that the mutation changes to or '-' in case of a deletion

position.description: The position in the reference genome where the mutation occurs

NucleotideSequencesResponse.description: An object containing the primary key and the requested sequence of a sample.

The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.
The parts of the fasta header to order by. By default, only the primary key and 'random' are allowed. The other fields are only allowed if they are part of the 'fastaHeaderTemplate'.

Limit.description: The maximum number of entries to return in the response

Offset.description:
The offset of the first entry to return in the response. 
    This is useful for pagination in combination with \"limit\".

SequencesDataFormat.description:
The format of the sequences in the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

TreeDataFormat.description: The format of the phylogenetic tree in the response.

NucleotideFastaHeaderTemplate.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.segment}" to include the segment name in the header.

AminoAcidFastaHeaderTemplate.description:
Use this to customize the header of the FASTA file.
This parameter will be ignored if the data format is not FASTA.
It supports free-form text, placeholders of the form "{<metadataField>}" to include metadata fields in the header and
"the keyword {.gene}" to include the gene name in the header.

DataFormat.description:
The data format of the response.
Alternatively, the data format can be specified by setting the \"Accept\"-header.
You can include the parameter to return the CSV/TSV without headers: "text/csv;headers=false". 
When both are specified, the request parameter takes precedence over the header.

error.description: Error message describing why parsing failed

QueriesOverTimeResult.description: The result in tabular format with queries as rows (outer array) and date ranges as columns (inner array).

dateRanges.description: The list of requested date ranges

data.description: A 2D array of query counts and coverage. It should be understood as data[index of query][index of date range].

totalCountsByDateRange.description: The list of total sample counts per date range

count.description: Number of sequences that match the 'countQuery' in the date range

coverage.description: Number of sequences that match the 'coverageQuery' in the date range. The query should be picked such that this number is the count of sequences that have a non-ambiguous symbol at the positions of interest.

count.description: Number of sequences with the mutation in the date range

coverage.description: Number of sequences with coverage (i.e., having a non-ambiguous symbol) at the position inthe date range. Confirmed deletions (i.e., "-") are included.

dateRanges.description: The list of requested date ranges

data.description: A 2D array of mutation counts and coverage. It should be understood as data[index of mutation][index of date range].
